page: Vultr vbm-64c-1536gb-amd is a bare metal server belonging to the Bare Metal NVMe family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9354 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The system features 64 physical cores and 128 threads with a base clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The CPU allocation is fully dedicated, ensuring no resource sharing with other tenants.

This server configuration includes 1536.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 12.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For local storage, the system is equipped with a 480 GB NVMe SSD. It does not include any graphics processing units. The network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

The server offers high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring large in-memory datasets without the overhead of virtualization. However, a notable tradeoff is the relatively small local storage capacity of 480 GB NVMe SSD, which may require external storage solutions for data-heavy applications.

Due to its high memory-to-core ratio and dedicated 128 vCPUs, this bare metal server is designed for memory-intensive workloads. Suitable use cases include large-scale in-memory databases, heavy virtualization hosting, enterprise resource planning systems, and complex data processing pipelines.
description: Vultr vbm-64c-1536gb-amd is a dedicated bare metal server from the Bare Metal NVMe family, featuring an AMD EPYC 9354 processor with 64 physical cores and 128 threads operating at 3.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server is equipped with 1536.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 12.0 GB per core, alongside a 480 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. It does not include any GPU resources. This configuration offers high resource density and cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads by eliminating virtualization overhead. However, the 480 GB local storage capacity represents a tradeoff for data-heavy deployments. This server is suited for large in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and memory-heavy data processing.

tagline: A dedicated bare metal server featuring 128 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 1.5 TB of RAM for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
